content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 1807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

August 1, 1978

Sally Abel, Coordinator
4th Senatorial District
Voter Education Fund
202 E Street
Davis, CA 95616

Dear Sally:

This will confirm the advice which I gave to you by
telephone on Monday, July 31, 1978.

Based on the information provided to the staff in
telephone conversations and in your letter dated July 26,
1978, it was the staff's conclusion that the 4th Senatorial
District Voter Education Fund should file a Statement of
Organization with the Secretary of State's Office as soon
as possible.

If you have any further questions, please contact
me or Regina Siciliano-Rutchins in our Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division.

Sincerely,

Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

BC:plh

4th Senatorial District Voter Education Fund
202 E Street
Davis, California 95616
(916) 753-3400

July 26,1978

Regina Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, 2nd Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Regina:

With regard to today's conversation I would
like to explain the purpose of the 4th Senatorial
District Voter Education Fund.

The 4th Senatorial District Voter Education
Fund is a non-partisan, independent committee
established to promote voter registration in
Sonoma, Napa, Solano, and Yolo counties.

The Fund has set a goal of 25,000 newly
registered voters within the four county area.
This will be accomplished by identification of
low registration precincts (or areas) through
analysis of registration and population figures.
Sampling and advice of local residents will also
be considered in determining priorities. Workers
will then be dispatched in targeted areas to do
door to door or shopping center registration.

The 4th Senatorial District Voter Education
Fund is currently soliciting contributions from
both political and non-political sources in order
to meet expected costs of the registration campaign.
A $1000 donation was received on July 18.

I hope the above information will be sufficient
in determining if the 4th Senatorial District Voter
Education Fund should file with the Secretary of
State as a Recipient Committee. Please advise me
should filing be necessary.

Sincerely,

Sally Abel
Sally Abel
Coordinator
